channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: HYPE weathered the storm, and is prepping for a parabolic rise in the weeks to come. — 54 replies — 132.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
HYPE weathered the storm, and is prepping for a parabolic rise in the weeks to come.

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-06-04 13:18 UTC

OP

HYPE weathered the storm, and is prepping for a parabolic rise in the weeks to come.

Early replies (first 3)

62309289
CZ lost

62309402
>>62309274
I used times ten leverage (x10) to buy one hundred (100) Hyperliquid coins (ticker: HYPE). My hyperliquidation price is sixty-six dot six-one-one (66.611) Circle american dollar tokens (USDC). Am I (anon) going to make it?

62309411
>>62309402
Idk what to say to you anon... just sell before 12th

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62311589
>>62311572
You're right. I really need to fix my gambling issues

62311611
>>62311526
stop loss exists

62311628
>>62311611
I am settings stop losses but I often have to fight myself to not remove them just before they get hit, because
>what if it just touches it then goes back?
How do you call this type of mental illness?

62311633
>>62311628
Meant to reply to >>62311526 as well

62311683
>>62311628
what if it keeps going and you lose everything? better to pay the ticket price 90% of the time instead of losing everything 90% of the time. this game is about preserving your capital

62311709
>>62311683
It's so hard with those damn scamwicks

62311778
>>62309274
Should I buy this gay semen coin or stick to fundamentals like BCH and LTC?

62311795
>>62311778
Looks like it still has gas in the tank. I'd buy if I were me.

62312318
>>62311795
I'm afraid it will act like ICP. Will dump when BTC pumps.

62312561
Holy shit what are you niggers even talking about? the OP is even MORE relevant with this days BTC dump and HYPE holding strong, even slightly gaining. Niggers should go all in HYPE right fucking NOW.

62312738
>>62311611
>>62311628
stop losses aren't reliable
it just means after the price is below, a market order happens. it doesn't mean it executes at your stop loss.
during severe market crashes you can get liquidated even with a "safe" stop loss

62312742
>>62309274
HYPECHADS won
linkjeets LOST

62312748
>>62310661
>>62311375
kek, you guys will be long gone by the time the next lunc happens. Whatever, continue your inorganic shill thread, anyone persistent enough to still come to this dead board isn't going to fall for anything this low effort.

62312807
>>62312748
I already know you won't answer and will probably continue with the buy an ad spam but what would you recommend I sell my hype bag for?

62313656
>>62309402
GG

62313668
>>62309402
thanks for this, seriously.

62313670
>>62309402
F

>>62309452
No. I'm the guy shorting BTC

>>62309478
Lmfao

>>62309533
Apologize.

If you guys knew, Hype is going to crash to 30 or even sub 30's, too many leverage positions.
https://www.coinglass.com/pro/futures/LiquidationHeatMap?coin=HYPE

And that's it guys, today's lesson is: don't buy alts in a bear market.

62313672
>>62312748
>inorganic shill thread
>only coin that's actually not totally dead

>>62312807
it's always an xrp fag or a link fag crying because he wants people to talk about his bags. No one else would give a shit.

62313678
>>62313670
>Apologize.
Blow me, you crying little linkjeet bitch.

62314017
Going to give it another day to see if it can bounce before I cut my losses

62314037
geg
solded
thanks for playing

62314084
>>62314037
Same. Will gamble some more when we hit $38-$42

62314107
Markets going parabolic. Super cycle phase. Force shorts to buy back or close shop.

62314115
>>62309274
So so fast baggies
>>62309402
Oooooffffffffffffff

62314119
>>62309402
And i mean generally ooooffffff and im sorry for you that you felt for the twatter scum shilling this at the top.

62314264
>>62313672
Hey buddy go ahead and pull up the HYPE chart for me.
>>62309402
Legendary.

62314273
That was the bottom

62314306
ADAMANTIUM DIAMOND ENCRUSTED SUPPORT AT $64

62314348
>>62309478
YOU LIED TO ME!!!!!!!

62314536
>>62309274
can i get actual commodities delivered using HYPE?

item.title: Actual insider here. I think this needs some clarification because Link marines have been waiting for almost 9 years now… — 49 replies — 110.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Actual insider here. I think this needs some clarification because Link marines have been waiting for almost 9 years now…

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-06-04 13:16 UTC

OP

Actual insider here. I think this needs some clarification because Link marines have been waiting for almost 9 years now and have been extremely patient. I can't really tell you too much because Chainlink Labs employees actually lurk biz (and post here) so they might able to know who I am. There has been an unexpected temporary delay of DTCC adoption due to the upgraded off-chain computation protocols (OCR 2.0) that had an unforeseen malfunction. However, the DONs seem to be fully online therefore the problem will be resolved quite rapidly.

In regards to SWIFT they actually seem to be fully operational by June. The main problem is that the network across EVM and non-EVM chains hasn't been fully tested to maximum capacity therefore releasing a product in such a short time would be quite a gamble for us. I'm confident enough that we'll be able to maintain a standardized interface to control the minimization protocol for the off-chain computation of the blockchain thus rendering the entire Chainlink ecosystem fully secured. At this rate I can only tell you that DTCC and SWIFT will be operational by the end of June, that's a certainty.

Strap yourselves in
It’s actually happening

Early replies (first 3)

62308409
AI post

62308412
>>62308361
>Actual insider here
Prove it

62308854
>>62308361
>wake up anon, new cope date just dropped
Thanks for the false hopium you beautiful larping nigger

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62309569
>>62309540
Sigh…We tried linkbro. We tried :/

62309577
mods, please get rid of the gay larps

62309590
>>62308361
That’s great but:
1. When staking v1
2. Price?
3. oh…. And chart?

62309594
>>62309540
The token has nothing to do with any of that though. The company will benefit, yes, but the token at this point is just a forgotten legacy artifact

62309604
>>62309594
All Chainlink products use the nodes, and the nodes must use the token for everything they do. No exceptions, ever.

62309667
>>62309540
126 trillion world economy. 8.1 trillion tokenized. $81,000 link price potential. 8.1 trillion is 15.5% of 126 trillion. 15.5% of $81,000 is $12,150 for Link price.

62309696
>>62308361
Based carrot dangler.

Just 2 more months and we're getting the carrot bros!

62309725
>>62309667
hey stinkie maybe focus first on getting back to your ATH from like 4 years ago rather than these insane delusions. your shitcoin cant even stay above 9 dollars

62309779
>>62309725
Do you understand what happens when payment abstraction gets switched on and a whole big bag of stables gets insta swapped for link? I’ll tell you. It means you get priced out, in seconds.

62309789
>>62309725
when i bought in 2019, never in my worst nightmares did i think the price would be $9 in 2026.

All news announcements and partnerships that doesn't effect price is equivalent to red hot torturers irons

62309813
>>62309789
Its because we're all living in hell and being tortured by elites for fun.

62309977
>>62308409
AI reply

62310043
>>62309141
what? you mean peepeepoopoo cuckold posts arent real fud?

62310082
>$8 dollarinos
stink will be worth $2 dollars at this rate by the end of june

62311031
>>62309292
how the fuck does the blockchain verify a pig's weight?

a weighing scales can do that. not the fucking blockchain

all the blockchain can do is save that information. but it can't verify that it reflects reality

62311046
>>62309779
Wen

62311473
>>62309779
Could you even imagine the salt of those selling at some point in the month before the flip is switched and it goes to $5,000 or something? I’m not even talking about 4chan people. I’m talking about some schmuck who just has like 300 Link for no reason other than he has a 50k portfolio with 8 different coins and has no emotional attachment to Link and never even thinks about it. And then bam… you just fucked yourself out of 1.5 million dollars. Thats way different than selling and it goes to $40 and then within 3 years it’s at $1,000 and missing out on a good chunk of change over a decent amount of time which is manageable.

62311642
>>62308361
I massively need this to enormously increase the price. But let's face it, it'll probably do fuck all, just like this >>62309779

Genuinely raging I didn't sell in 2021 as I would've been retired.

62311722
with every year, link marines bump the numbers they imagine the token will be to make up for all the lost time. it's too late for them, they will never sell, making their initial a donation to chainlink

62311909
>>62311642
Just 2 more years until Sergey lets us have the carrot he's been dangling in front of us. Hang tight!

62311939
>>62311909
The thing is that they’re building/developing and delivering. No one else comes close. If Link fails everything fails.

62311940
fuddies on suicide watch again

62311944
>>62311940
p + c, dumb stanker

62311996
>>62311939
They're delivering for CLL (the company), not the token holders. Token holders are thrown scraps in the form of the strategic reserve

62312010
>>62311944
c(ope) + s(needthe), detractor

62312018
>>62309779
>Payment abstraction gets switched on
>Stables get instant swapped for link...
>And then immediately get instant swapped for stables as soon as they land
You retards do realize this means nothing happens right? There's a reason people pay their AWS bills in USD and Bezos didn't decide to make his own Amazon Bucks to pay for the services in, right?

62312247
>>62312018
you do realize that it's discounted to pay in link?

62312338
>>62308854
At first it was for the pump and dump.
Nowadays it might be for senseless torture.
Or maybe some are testing some AI to see how much it is efficient at scamming people.

62313737
>>62312247
because it's the inferior payment method, have to advertise it somehow. yet everyone still pays with stables

62314528
>>62313737
wrong, it incentivizes link purchasing.

item.title: No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this. — 105 replies — 89.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this.

Open thread • Replies: 105 • Updated: 2026-06-04 12:32 UTC

OP

Saylor sells 32 BTC out of 843,000+ BTC and anon starts acting like Bitcoin is dead.

You're not a real BTC holder.

Early replies (first 3)

62299202
why did he sell 32 btc tho?

62299205
>>62299202
to get a higher rating from some corporate wagie on wall street. he was showing his btc holdings are unencumbered and can be sold at will.

62299325
Didn't he tell people to never sell?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62312873
>>62308212
You're kind of lumping everything together there. Babylon isn't the same thing as parking your BTC on a centralized yield platform.

62312879
>>62299187
stop questioning the narrative

62312888
>>62302278
It is (was..) Saylor's USP, NEVER to sell.
Without which, hes Just Another Asshole.

62312894
>>62299647
Maybe, but hindsight is always 20/20. At $200, nobody knew how the story would play out.

62313133
>>62308253
You can't just say that and expect people not to check lol. Especially now that the TBV public testnet is live. A lot of people are trying it out.

62313137
>>62299824
Luna 2.0? Damn, you're dreaming yeah?

62313144
>>62299854
That analogy only works if one buyer is the entire market. Bitcoin is a global asset with millions of participants.

62313152
>>62312315
No real bitcoiners still hold and if they did for ideological reasons they'd hold Monero

62313157
>>62299564
I'd argue it's better to move to private, decentralized coins. Nerva is a solid one that can't even be pool mined and is only mineable with a CPU. During this crypto downturn, it's actually been trending UP!

62313239
bitcoiners are rotating into zcash. near swap volume doesn't lie.

62313245
>>62313133
I don't understand what you're talking about

62313253
>>62313157
If you're into privacy, monero is better than nerva

62313260
>>62313239
monero ser

62313269
>>62313260
will never be mainstream like BTC or ZEC are.

62313275
>>62313269
What's nerva

62313321
He has burned through 12 months of cash in 6 months. He will have 0 cash on hand in about two months at the current rate if he doesn't sell any bitcoin to offset it. What he will probably do is jackup the yield even more which means he will have to sell even more bitcoin each month to cover it (already $150M a month atm)

62313462
>>62313245
I meant the new TBV public testnet. It's basically a chance to try native BTC-backed borrowing through Aave V4 anon.

62313465
>>62303647
We never know their plans

62313476
>>62303658
I don't think anyone really knows how the market would react until it's actually tested.

62313530
>>62305074
His influence is hard to ignore, but I don't think one player completely defines the market.

62313567
>>62313530
What happens when every major holders sell their btc

62313591
>>62313462
If you hold btc, hold. Don't do anything risky. Defi is no longer safe.

62313595
>>62306852
NICE FUD

62313964
>>62313591
but I think the quality of the protocol matters a lot. Some projects go through multiple independent audits before launch, so I wouldn't put every DeFi protocol in the same category.

62313972
>>62313321
Maybe. I don't think it's as straightforward as that, but it'll be interesting to see how it plays out.

62313977
>>62307496
Market buys would have been a lot more entertaining, that's for sure.

62314023
>>62299824
Why do you say that?

62314048
>>62312315
Whether people like Saylor or not, Bitcoin itself doesn't really care who owns it.

62314204
>>62299187
He's about to slurp up the remaining 157k

62314317
Saylor sold a pittance of his bitcoins (32) to crash the market, now he's going to swoop in and buy even more, the man is playing 5D chess.

item.title: Germany is such a corrupt shithole — 14 replies — 28.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Germany is such a corrupt shithole

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-06-04 13:13 UTC

OP

Now they are forcing UBER to almost double their prices because taxi drivers demanded it (since their prices were so expensive nobody would use them anymore). So now all we have is absurd UBER prices.

Early replies (first 3)

62308549
>>62308545
just walk u lazy krautnigger

62308551
>>62308545
>NOOOO this amerikanische company must make money off us and fleece its workers!!!

62308590
>>62308545
uber revolutionized taxis for me
before uber i was utterly loathe to take a taxi
>ring the taxi company
>background noise
>some mumbling arab on the phone
>spitting at you
>doesn't even appear to want your custom
>no idea what the price will be
>no idea how long it will take
>taxi driver is probably an illegal
but now it is consistently a great experience and simply how taxis should be
>put in my exact location and destination
>told exactly what it will cost
>told exactly where the driver is
>told exactly what vehicle they are in
>drivers have a rating so cars are always clean and they are always pleasant
>plus there are tips
>riders also have a rating and drivers are given discretion about who they want to take
i honestly struggle to think of any other industry which has been so much improved by a singular app
it's not good that uber has such a monopoly but taxi companies deserved this by being so shit that they couldn't compete
if uber really was just added cost then it wouldn't be competitive but not only is it more convenient and more pleasant it isn't even more expensive
i don't believe for one moment that it is actually worse for most drivers either because before they needed a taxi agency which undoubtedly siphoned their revenues whereas now they are essentially independent under uber

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62308626
>>62308549
I unironically do
>>62308551
YOU FUCKING RETARD PEOPLE LIKE YOU ARE THE PROBLEM
They were offering the same service for cheaper. CLEARLY they were making less money then the corrupt taxxi assholes. And muh poor workers, they earn their normale wage, give them extra if you want. If they would earn so little they would just ask for Bürgergeld (social welfare). So clearly they were the far more efficient service compared to that old bullshit taxi system.
>>62308590
Yes exactly and the german government hates on it since the very start and put out media campaigns against it, and now they are jsut trying to bruteforce UBER out.

62308629
>>62308590
>i don't believe for one moment that it is actually worse for most drivers either because before they needed a taxi agency which undoubtedly siphoned their revenues whereas now they are essentially independent under uber
Oh yeah this system is max extraction, and the government takes massive share with licences and shit.

62308644
>>62308629
German here. take as much as you can from the gov and give them nothing. dont work (schwarzarbeit is ok). Stop saving/using euro so inflation tax wont work. That way we can actually make a difference by not giving this hostile shit government money.

62308751
>>62308590
lel the cattle ladies and gentlemen
>>62308629
you VILL pay for the uber license

62308768
>>62308551
If Americans making money is the problem, then ban UBER but allow equivalent European alternatives

The reason drivers are "fleeced" is because the there's an abundance of people who can drive a car, so drivers have little negotiating leverage.

62308823
>>62308768
>abundance of people who can drive a car
allowed to yes, can no

62311555
>>62308626
>If they would earn so little they would just ask for Bürgergeld (social welfare).
You think the illegals aren't leeching off welfare? Lol

>>62308768
>so drivers have little negotiating leverage.
They have little negotiating leverage since Uber claims to just be a middle man linking you to an independent driver that has to get paid through them. That way they can circumvent some fees, taxes and social security.

62312332
>>62311555
Checked. So they dont actually get fleeced if they collect welfare aswell. Only the government. Which is not my problem.

62314310
>>62308626
Oh no, you have to accept it when a big hostile company uses shareholder money to crush all the competition via price dumping, hires illegal workers from India who do not pay taxes, and then when all the competition has given up increases the prices in its newly created monopoly in something as innovative as a taxi service.

Only smart people can run a taxi service you must accept our smart American (((innovation)))

62314337
>>62308768
There is also an abundance of people who can operate an app like Uber.

The thing is that Uber uses stakeholder money to crush the competition and to create a monopoly.

No productive value is generated by this kind of capitalism.

Also, a lot of people in Germany who work for Uber or Delivery Hero are working illegally and were scammed into debt by being promised a job at a big German company and then they have to work gigs 12 hours a day. It is basically like slavery.

62314512
>>62308545
If you run a restaurant and have to follow all the restaurant regulations but then someone create a app and have random people just cooking in their kitchen with no food safety i think you would have a problem with that as well
Even if you might disagree with some of the regulations
Taxi drivers have to follow certain rules and regulations and it's part of why the price is higher and they don't want to be undercut by uber

item.title: If LINK is already breaking into the $7s, how low will it be in Bottomtober — 4 replies — 12.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
If LINK is already breaking into the $7s, how low will it be in Bottomtober

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-04 13:24 UTC

OP

If LINK is already breaking into the $7s, how low will it be in Bottomtober

Early replies (first 3)

62314487
$200

62314548
0

62314550
$0.70

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62314566
>>62314479
$2

item.title: WHERE IS THE FUCKING SUPPORT — 11 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WHERE IS THE FUCKING SUPPORT

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-04 12:47 UTC

OP

WHERE IS THE FUCKING SUPPORT

Early replies (first 3)

62314186
>>62314181
Just don't look at the price nigglet, it's that easy

62314190
What support?

62314206
SUPPORT SITS at 20K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62314266
>>62314181
no where to be found

62314272
>>62314181
We haven't even double-bottomed yet and you're crying for 'support saaaar'
lol...lmao even

62314274
>>62314272
Where is our fucking money lawyer man we want out 100k pay out now !

62314321
I JUST BOUGHT BACK IN AT THE BOTTOM!!!!!!! HERE WE GO

62314344
>>62314181
Seems like we found it at 62k. Same place we had a few months ago

62314356
>>62314344
Unironically bullish if this forms a double-bottom at a higher level on the daily.

62314364
>>62314181
PHULL SAPPORT SAAR

62314392
Max pain. I'm sluping until i'm rich or broke.

item.title: today another $1mil link will be bought for the link reserve, its so bullish fellow link marines, ever since the launch… — 3 replies — 7.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
today another $1mil link will be bought for the link reserve, its so bullish fellow link marines, ever since the launch…

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-06-04 13:18 UTC

OP

today another $1mil link will be bought for the link reserve, its so bullish fellow link marines, ever since the launch we've seen an extreme amount of positive news and huge increase in the link reserve buys, CLL is a real pioneer in showing appreciation for those who hold/buy/stake their token, you will all make it.

62314384
please don't sell

62314402
>>62314384
I wont, you dont need to ask that of me everyday

I will never sell

62314538
>>62314402
He wasn’t asking

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

this is a reminder to

B U Y
U
Y

T H E
H
E

D I P
I
P

bharat is well on its way to becoming a SUPER POWER by 2030, you either invest NOW or be left behind forever sirs

FACT: india went to the moon, more times than the US in the last 5 year
FACT: india won a war with pakistan in less than 24 hours
FACT: india is projected to be the fastest groaning economy in 2300
FACT: when china collapses india will take all of their economy and become even stronger

if yo uarent LONG BHARAT then you are the permanent under class

Early replies (first 3)

62312569
>>62312560
indiaGODs won. Timmies will fade this as their own women choose superior brahmin cock

62312607

TRVKE facts !

62314385
good morning saars, another fine day to investing bharat

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 36 replies — 92.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-06-04 13:19 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62312779
>>62312653
im a fuddie link was due for its last little green midget candle against bitcoin and this was it.

every little grrn cndle pump gets yall uephoric


yall should sell now unironically.

ill screenshot this so i cn remind yall again later

62312833
and just like that linkjeets will never rise above $8 dollars ever again

62312857
>>62312833
say it with me: pain and misery are their ONLY friends xD

62312859
>>62312833
they will in the next bull run in a few years and it will hit 17

then under 5 dollars by 2030 after every last link marine has invested everything they had into their cult

all of crypto is like this, every coin

62312862
>>62312614
even some retarded normie in my office has 1k link. this shit is going nowhere.

62312881
it's April 2026 and there's a link thread on the front page
it's a problem of neuroplasticity. you're supposed to be able to evaluate new ideas
the fact link is still remembered is bizarre. it's just another worthless token from the first ethereum ico wave
it's like being stuck in dragonchain

62312891
is being stuck a consequence of being on this board?
you have people who regularly make fun of linkies. this creates a tribal identity of "I'm a linkie, I'm not selling"
feedback loop of autism that lasts for years and leads to financial ruin

62312913
>>62312881
>>62312891
If you actually pay even a modicum of attention to crypto you know that LINK is one of less than 5 out of millions of cryptos seeing constant adoption. One of less than 5 actually relevant tokens. And you are here spewing this. Being you must be very sad.

62312945
>>62312913
does adoption = price crash? cuz if so, miss me on that

62313055
>>62312913
And yet adoption has done nothing for actual holders, because like people have been warning you for years, there's no connection between what CLL is doing with backroom cash deals and the price of the coin.

62313428
Ka-BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM
link

62313440
>>62312614
Regulation my nigga

62313674
>>62312653
>LINK/BTC trading at 2017 levels
congratulations cuckold

62313687
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62313738
>>62312945
>does adoption = price crash?

Chainlink adoption = Bitcoin price crash

62313745
>>62313738
Chainlink adoption = Sergey gets rich = cuckold baggies get fucked

You got that?

62313758
>>62313745
You've been saying this for years now.
Either you're still holding Link yourself, or you somehow benefit from spamming the same lines for years.
Which is it?

62313762
haha, linkies are losing money

62313764
the future of finances is ruining their finances haha

62313768
>>62313762
>linkies are losing money
In the last 7 days Link is doing better than almost the entire top 20.

62313788
>>62313758
I'm getting paid by Simeon, what are you gonna do about it Stinkshitter?

62313866
>>62312614
hey OP, i'm a link holder from the presale we did here, let me explain as simply as possible why the price doesn't appreciate even if we get all those partnerships, people on X call me a fuddie, I mostly "FUD" is it really fudding when you're trying to get the team to move their asses? i don't know, but blindly supporting the team in everything they do is stupid as hell, i prefer to "FUD" all the time even though it hurts my bags, btw the only crypto i hold and i'm all in in, is LINK, i got a fat stack fully staked in multiple wallets since the 0.1.

here is the simple reason why we don't pump:
the fucking SCALE PROGRAM.

why? first, let me explain what SCALE is, scale basically is a deal between chainlink LABS (the company) and institutional clients, where in exchange for big lumps of money, chainlink labs will cover all the the chainlink network costs using their pre-minted "locked" link all while diluting us (the holders), the institution will also pay for all the chainlink fees in exchange for this. basically, the scale program is "we pay for you using our preminter shitcoins, while you pay us", i've discussed with many team members including coventry, thomas and zach the chainshitgod, and they all agree that cll makes hundreds of millions of $ worth of money through the scale program, and this shit will continue increasing, the DTCC or swift also have private contracts through the company called SmartContract.com which employes 9 employees and sergey is the ceo you can go and search smart contract company on linked in.

this fucking company is what funds chainlink labs, and basically this company will also offer the chainlink network for literally FREE to their clients by selling them locked link through agreements made.

sergey is smart and a good guy, but he thinks we are dumb fucks, cll and smartcontract.com are acting like they own the network, like it's their private property ...1/2

62313871
>>62313866

they can sell to their clients using preminted tokens, think about it for a second, it's as if the ethereum foundation covered the eth gas fees of some of their clients if they pay them money (i know that the foundation already steals from corporate they already ask for 8% i believe) but that's not the subject here.

i think us linkies we should pressure CLL about this bullshit because they are effectively stealing from the network, and this shit can continue for a looong timmme

62313883
>>62313866
>>62313871
Would you rather have CLL doing nothing like every other crypto project?

Atleast they are pulling in institutions and customers and eventually premine will run out

62313887
>>62313883
if it runs out, they will create another token. linkies will buy it. it will be require "for security reasons"

62313910
>>62313883
it's me I just switched to my phone, brother, listen at 9$ the total rewards we are getting throught staking (the 4.3% of the 40.8M link staked) is worth 15M$, can't you see that the staking rewards are just a fucking carrot? they are not only stealing from the network (hundreds of millions of $) but also they are diverting some of that money from the stakers through the reserve, they are "buybacking" like 1.1M$ every week of tokens, think about it, there are 52 weeks in a year, so the reserve is getting like 4 times more link than the stakers, they are literally refusing to pay us, they rather store the link in some fake ass "reserve than distribute it to stakers, they give us a total of 15M$ yearly while putting 60M in the reserve and keeping about 340M yearly to themselves.
can a fucking company get more greedy than this?

62313954
>>62313910
also i forgot to mention some of the deals they got like the 1.2% GMX fees they literally memory holed that shit, nobody heard from it since and we don't know where that money is going, add to that the fucking build program, where a project gives em a share of their shitcoin and they also subsidize the network for them using the preminted tokens.
the cll company is diverting like more than 100% of the revenue the network genrates by having private deals, and ontop of that using the preminted shitcoins to increase their own company revenue and paying the node operators, i sriously hope an influencer or a journalist or a podcaster sees my posts and fucking shames cl labs so they move their asses and stop taking us fir the stuffed turkey, because they are only giving us the staking rewards so we stfu and keek being the good cucks that we are

62314036
>>62313883
also let me address the "the premined tokens will runout" i'm sorry i sound like a pro fudder but i'm an INTP i think fucking alot, they won't run out of tokens, xanna know why? because th/'s what the strategic reserve is, can you imagine how smart this is? when they take hundreds of millions of $ through the private deals, they also DCA 1.1 million $ every week rebuying the tokens they used to subsidize, what do you think the reserve is for? it's so they will continue this ponzi forever, like they will take in millions as $, then rebuy some of the tokens basically DCA over years, then redo this shit once their reserve are gonna almost run out, they will use thz reserve by crashing th3 shitcoin and rebuying thzir tokens then say oh the reserves purpose is to subsidize the network and pay for the clients, dont you find it weird that they are already subsidizing the costs and fees for their clients all while having a reserve? they are simply risk managing the tokens running out buy buying low and so the cycle never ends.
i dunno why i have never seen such conclusions by any fuddie or linky alike, i dont like the fud but arn't those just facts?

62314063
>>62313883
>eventually premine will run out
why is that even relevant?
Off the top of my head I can't think of a single crypto whose premine ran out, yet crypto still did what it did.

62314544
>>62313910
Yea I don’t really understand why stakers aren’t getting paid that money. Even if they stopped the 4% gibs and swapped to paying out what they’re keeping for themselves it 3x’s staking rewards. And the actual system they promised is in place to some degree. Nobody cares if that shit is automated. Just pay our stakers our just dues. We have been staking for what like 5 fucking years for peanuts and watching our portfolio melt? We truly are the cuckolds of crypto.

item.title: 65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC — 41 replies — 22.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-06-04 11:51 UTC

OP

>disregard dwarves
>disregard doomerfags
>if you bought the rally, hold
>if you didn't, enjoy the discount

Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value. You can buy groceries with BTC, you can't buy it with rock collections or shitcoins. And ever less the fiat bullshit can buy groceries.

>but BTC is dumping to zero--

Zoom out.

Bitcoin is the future of currency and if you haven't realized yet, you are either a fool, a scammer, or a paid shill.

Buy the fucking dip and stop selling the bottom.

‐ Yours truly, the BUYROG, pmetalsbane.

Early replies (first 3)

62309508
>>62309503
proof??

62309515
This thread now is the 64k waiting room

62309535
>>62309508
I don't need proof. I made money for everyone who bought the previous dip, who were considering cashing out and start a rock collection. And this time it isn't going to be any fucking different.

ETF returns are pathetic. PM is not investment. Bonds are tanking.

Buy BTC.

Let's get rich together.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62311890
bottom, now.

62311921
I’m gonna laugh so hard when Gaylor buys 200 btc tomorrow

62312109
>>62311487
>>62311869
>>62311890
>>62311487
>HE SOLD THE BOTTOM
HE SOLD THE BOTTOM
>HE SOLD THE BOTTOM
HE SOLD THE BOTTOM

At this point, if you sell BTC, I thank you for doing so because no asset class survives while held by the financially illiterate. Just look at PMs for a very grim example.

I insist on my position, and you can see the decline in price is already slowing down. Tomorrow, it'll normalize around 66k, then it'll be near 80k by the end of the month.

It is always like this. You spend ten fucking years talking about buying the bottom, then when the actual bottom is hit, you don't take the risk.

Just buy bonds and index at this point, seriously, if you still fumble the clearest bottom in history.

62312124
>>62311921
>I’m gonna laugh so hard when Gaylor buys 200 btc tomorrow

That nigga is leveraged to the max, he already had to sell to pay debts, spending 12 million after paying a 180k debt isn't likely

62312131
>>62309503
>bottom is 65k
kek no its around 45k-50k

62312142
>>62311471
Don’t move, this thread is the 62k waiting room now

62312143
>>62312142
>mfw I wake up and it's 59k

62312204
>>62312143
Still too high

62312220
>>62312142
>>62312143
>>62312204
The permanent underclass chimes in.

The Balrog has never been wrong about BTC. Ever. It always calls the bottom.

62312590
>>62312220
It’s been less than two hours and we’ve already reached 62k, jej

62312595
>>62312142
That happened way faster than I was expecting

62312604
Rainbow pattern!

62312613
Rainbows all the way!

62312656
>>62312595
Americans just got back from work and started dumping after seeing the mess left by institutional and Euro traders

62313701
>>62309503
>Bitcoin is the future of currency
I do not want to hold currency, I want to hold values that I need to live, and diversified shares in upcoming industries. Currency I hold very briefly, for a few weeks, to buy food and stuff.

62313706
>>62309503
61k btw, -6% below the bottom, within hours, 12% below last cycle top, in the spring of the bear year. I smell saturation.

62313723
kek baggie

keep spamming /biz/ maybe Bitcoin will pump by +1%

62313736
>>62309503
>stateless
lol/lmao
did not read

62313815
BTC is now lower than the 2021 top

62313877
>>62313815
>BTC is now lower than the 2021 top


TOP Signal
>20K will be the bottom this year!!

62313967
>>62312142
Well that room got closed when I got there. Seems you are in charge here. Where do I go now?

62314072
>>62313967
Please take a seat in the 60k waiting room and enjoy a delicious free drink.

62314074
65k is my bottom nice long wick on the monthly.

62314089
>>62309559
Bobo is your friend without him everything would be too expensive.

62314092
>>62311487
Building momentum.

62314094
I feel bad for Bitkiddies. They need to always make up a new narrative like iem weirdo shilling you his product on the street and hope you have a 2 year memory so you don't remember all the all the other ways he tried to shill this product to you.


Meanwhile with Gold, all you need to do is look at the news, read world bank headlines and just the general macros that haven't changed in forever. I don't even feel sad or mad at our Bitkiddies anymore. Just say "I have no money" whole walking into the gold miner store.

62314100
>>62314094
Dude youre married on a porn site go reload that shill else where

62314120
>>62314072
The interesting thing is that it broke 75k so easily end of January, so I sold the bounce. I thought 75 is the new Trump value added bottom. But it just teared through it like nothing, now it is almost at the usual bottom just below the last cycle top, weekly chart mostly maxed out, in the most bullish stock market that ever existed. Like all the institutional ideas are of zero value. It seems people really looked at saturation, or found that there aren't any rich buyer groups left to scam into buying the next top.

62314163
>>62314100
Isn't it weird how your "money" is going to be hacked by quantum computers in under 3 years?

62314177
um

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 112 replies — 13.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 112 • Updated: 2026-06-04 05:21 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62302104
>>62301542
>>62301440
We'll see - the hit rate's 78%, not 100%. The general sense, per the twitter thread yesterday, is bounce to the 5th-7th, then drop to the real bottom mid-June and then pump through summer, but obviously Brownian motion can't predict Saylor selling lol

62303698
Tell me no one bought because of this indicator please HAHAHAHAHA

62303805
Is this some intraday trading indicator? Like you were supposed to buy and then sell with leverage the day of the OP? If you would have bought and were holding now you would be fucked

62304332
reverse indicator of op is printing me money

62305278
Look how they massacred my coin

62305335
>>62296711
I appreciate the insight. This thread has been an interesting read.

62305363
>>62305335
Thanks Anon, wish it was going better right now though!
Liquidity's good, the sim says "increasing demand," sure enough stocks are up and copper/gold is acting like a boom is coming, but crypto is just getting slaughtered. The only explanation I can think of is we're getting fucked now that CME crypto futures are legal. But without any actual data that's probably just cope

62305385
it's over

62306031
>>62305363
I had an asset do 200% in like 7 days recently, so it seems at least that people aren't turning up their noses at it all, so long as substantial developments are being made. Still not immune to dipping after considering profit-taking. Good projects can stand out in a storm like a lighthouse does. We'll see if that continues into the year.

62306711
>>62306031
Oh, I don't think crypto's over at all, unless someone has cracked SHA-256. Something is definitely happening to the asset class, though. A pro-crypto Fed chair just took over, stocks are up, but our internet coins are acting like they're about to be banned (for the tenth time)

62308784
Bump

62308863
>>62285441
You can trade at mexc and make maker orders close to the current price, that way only the taker pays 0.4-5% and you lose nothing.

62309487
>>62306031
Consistency is key.

62310796
>>62308784
I think we're fully in a news-driven cycle now, or maybe more accurately a institutional-crime-driven cycle. Physics-liquidity has no edge rn.
I'm working on this seasonality model which has caught some pivots so far, and if it's right we trend down to mid-June and then pump hard through the summer, but it's extremely experimental and I'll need months more results to compare to predictions before I could even start tuning it

62310807
>>62310796
it could also be the observer effect has broken your model

62310837
>>62310807
>the observer effect has broken your model
If Saylor's found my model he could at least send me a couple shekels
That said, about 95 times out of 100 a physics-predicted pivot that happens on a weekend gets frontrun the last trading day before, which does make me suspect institutions also figured this out

62310897
>>62285204
>the thing has a ~80% hit rate over more than a year

80% of the time it works all of the time

62310926
based

62311911
Bro..?

62313065
>>62311911
Boomer stocks keep doing what they're supposed to but crypto's getting killed = this isn't market-driven, we're getting fucked

62313095
>>62285468
Tell me how to day trade using the moon phases

62313156
I want to use this but I fear I am incredibly stupid. Also why give this out for free? Isn’t the number 1 advice to keep an edge for yourself if you find one? This seems like too good to be true

62313227
>>62313156
>Also why give this out for free? Isn’t the number 1 advice to keep an edge for yourself if you find one?
It's a multi trillion dollar asset class, or at least it used to be. We're not going to move it
>I want to use this but I fear I am incredibly stupid.
Buy on confirmed green/sell on confirmed red/derisk on confirmed yellow, there's live gauges plus confirmation/invalidation on the site, and set a SL so you don't get rekt like this week when the system signaled "Buy" and everything else went up but crypto got drowned in a lake

62313294
>he's still going
Fucking based.

>>62313065
>"boomer stocks"
AI stocks literally move as fast as crypto used to lmao. This board loves Hype (or maybe 'love' is a strong word, but the 'it's gonna be the next 100 billion crypto in 2027' posters are shilling it hard) but hype is only up 4x from the lows. Meanwhile SNDK was 230 bucks in December and is now 1800 bucks. SNXX, the LETF, went up 8x from launch. The Micron LETF was 50 bucks in december and 1000 bucks now- literally 50k in that would be 1m in 5 months.

62313308
>>62313227
sorry im a bit retarded here. so you're saying this bounce continues for a few days, then back down and then pump into summer?

62313309
>>62313227
What does volume reduction mean in your trade simulator? Also that simulator is still way in beta right? Cuz according to it lower SL is always better, 0% SL bringing the best results. And leverage what already talked about but damn I thought I could become buffet starting with my 500$ portfolio for a sec

62313323
>>62313294
>AI stocks literally move as fast as crypto used to lmao.
Sometimes I wish I was in the timeline where I rotated all my ETH into NVDA in 2021. I did enough that it doesn't look like I'll need a job again, but maybe it'd be nice to have my own island

>>62313309
>Also that simulator is still way in beta right? Cuz according to it lower SL is always better, 0% SL bringing the best results. And leverage what already talked about but damn I thought I could become buffet starting with my 500$ portfolio for a sec
Extremely in beta. I added all the options (leverage, SL etc) a week ago and it's clearly fucked up the SL math. Big update's coming mid-month and it'll be fixed by then at the latest
>What does volume reduction mean in your trade simulator?
It's for the derisk on a confirmed Volatility signal. The demo strategy, in the t just cuts to 50% cash. Ofc you could just move stops up or whatever suits your own trading style

62313334
>>62313323
How much are you gonna charge for it when it’s done and how long do you think that’ll take?

62313351
>>62313334
>How much are you gonna charge for it when it’s done and how long do you think that’ll take?
Nowhere near happy enough with the site to even think about charging money for it. I'll charge for API access and the trade bot when that's done, probably this summer. Maybe a subscription for a TG channel with specific price signals? Dunno, that's all way in the future

62313359
>>62313351
And "confirmed buy/sell" means that FXwave confirms the already marked signal on the given day if I’m understanding correctly?

item.title: Serious link thread - pt8 — 22 replies — 12.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Serious link thread - pt8

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-06-04 11:56 UTC

OP

https://warosu.org/biz/thread/58439987
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/58717206
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/58917732
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/59166963
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/59237592
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/61359310
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/61380724

anon, how are you holding up? its been a while friend. i hope you have been DCAing. in my last thread, i talked about not seeing the current region we are in without serious systemic issues. between then and now, US president has kidnapped a leader of a foreign nation and caused a major spike in oil by escalating conflict with iran. neither were on my bingo card

on top of that, clarity act delayed. the banks have lost, but they are fully capable of arguing approval for as long as possible. my initial expectation was Q1, july 4th has been floated as an idea, and even late Q4 now

we are at the top of 2022/2023 resistance bands. dont panic, but there is a play here for $5 link with continued political turmoil. i dont expect it to drop that far. at the end of the day, when link is $1k+, did it matter if you bought at ICO, 5, 8, 12, or 50? maybe it does to prove you were more right than the next guy. dont lose sight of why you came across this coin, its the one to protect you and your family from the future hellscape

you should be completely desensitized to politics at this point. the democrat and republican parties are both facades for new and old school billionaires and their posturing in the market for more power. trumps a pawn, they all are pawns. clarity act is being used the same way biden used DACA, this bill is gatekeeping crypto from being applied for real use cases and tokenization of financial markets instead of crypto just being animal coins preying on hype. large link wallets have continued to accumulate links. follow smart moneys play here and lower your average cost basis

Early replies (first 3)

62313124
>>62313123
my guess, clarity act, price of oil, and current situation with iran are all being dangled and will be resolved before november 3rd mid terms. clarity act passes Q3, special scenario of it passing ~july 4th to compensate for the current US anniversary shitshow. if you arent a neet, you should know your company is debating the usage of AI coins and the fact that paying a person to solve something is starting to become more cost effective than haggling with AI for hours and spending the tokens. money flows back to crypto. remember how aggressive 2021/2022s run up was? people have more money on the sidelines right now, and if they dont, they will make it available. except this time, people wont be investing into crypto based on hype, but based on tokenization usage

im on edge, keep DCAing, find pennies in the couch cushions. and dont forget, when we win, what you learned here. dont be a freak like epstein

62313128
WHY IS IT DIPPING TO $7
YOU STUPID FUCKS TOLD ME IT'D BE $10K THIS IS FUCKING INSANE!!!! FUCK YOU GOD DAMNED INDIAN SCAMMERS

62313134
>>62313128
Because it's going to sub zero.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62313136
link sucks link sucks fuck stinks hate em all simple as

62313174
I like chainlink

62313184
idk, the tech seems good. in fairness tho i'm a tech guy

62313223
>>62313184
explain to us how the tech is good, tech guy.
this oughta be good for a laugh

62313417
>>62313184
tech guy guy here, can confirm

62313432
Just... Remember... to...

Smile, when your heart is aching
Smile, even though it's breaking

KaBOOOOOOOOOM
Link

62313434
>>62313123
>anon, how are you holding up?
bad, I've lost almost everything. oh well. I don't think clarity will pass in july, unfortunately they go into recess at the end of june, so barring special circumstances, they won't even consider it until july, and once again I doubt they'll pass it before the recess. once it clears the senate keep in mind it has to go back to the house where this process can continue being retarded. I'd like to believe they will pass it this year, but who knows. It's still debatable whether it's even good for crypto at all. the biggest real problem isn't the banks or the political climate, but the entrenched crypto powers that control the supply of basically every token (chainlink included) and who are content to just dump forever. I really think crypto has failed as an idea.

62313437
>>62313128
buckle up, we're going to $0.77 by EOM

62313438
where we at eom fellas? what's june got in store?
I'm so excited I cant wait!

62313571
How tf should I lower my DCA lol? Only through staking is it possible but Jonny keeps hogging the pool entries

62313575
>>62313123
>>62313124
>>62313128
>>62313134
>>62313136

KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

SERIOUS LINK THREAD

62313578
>>62313174
>>62313184
>>62313223
>>62313417
>>62313432

So fxcking bullish chainlink

BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62313580
Dont think it really matters

Whole world and economy seems to be crashing

62313582
A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn $1 per 1 LINK per DAY through staking. Does that mean if I’ve staked 2000 LINK, I’d earn $2000 per day?
Is that true, or was the anon just talking bullshit?

62313718
>>62313123
>at the end of the day, when link is $1k+
How long are you deranged cuckolds going to spam this nonsense? It's been 9 years keeeek

62314182
>>62313123
I’m using this dump in prices to accumulate more

62314192
>>62314182
based and cuckoldpilled

OG Marines always knew Chainlink was a 20 year hold.

62314197
>>62313123
I'm a 2017 1k suicide baggot. Bought some more here and there but I was in law school so couldn't manage much. I should have but all my covid money in, though the price was really high then.

I still believe and chainlink and #neverselling. I should probably look in to staking desu, missing out on a lot of link. Is open at this point?

Anyways maybe I'll buy some more, seems like a good time. I'm just making way too much money on stonks...

62314200
>>62313718
I will simply remain delusional longer than market will remain irrational

item.title: VOO Becomes the First $1 Trillion ETF — 11 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO Becomes the First $1 Trillion ETF

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-04 12:47 UTC

OP

DCA into VOO.
https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/voo-becomes-first-1-trillion-000450793.html

Early replies (first 3)

62313593
>>62313590
I've been DCA'ng into VOO for the past 10 years

62313692
>>62313590
how dumb do you have to be to keep funding kikes and their globohomo kikery?

62313716
>>62313692
just say youre a brokenigger thats holding crypto bags

item.title: >it's the Bulgarians from NEXO >its the Bitcoin whales >its Adem and the Twitter gooners >its Layerzero >its Jump Tradin… — 6 replies — 10.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
>it's the Bulgarians from NEXO >its the Bitcoin whales >its Adem and the Twitter gooners >its Layerzero >its Jump Tradin…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-04 13:09 UTC

OP

>it's the Bulgarians from NEXO
>its the Bitcoin whales
>its Adem and the Twitter gooners
>its Layerzero
>its Jump Trading

Gosh the cuckolds are so hilarious, can't wait to see their shitcoin crash back to 2018 prices

Early replies (first 3)

62313902
rent free

62313916
>>62313902
you're poor lol

62313925
>>62313916
wow that's super low, wasn't it like double digits a while ago? what happened?

62313927
>>62313925
turns out the fuddies were right and Chainlink is a scam. TNN wasn't a meme

62314347
>>62313927
